title: Opportunity to fly in rear seat of Spitfire from Prestwick

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: A once-in-a-lifetime opportunity to fly in the rear seat of a Spitfire from Prestwick Airport will cost at least £8,000. As part of the aircraft's 90th birthday, a series of flights are being held up and down the country to mark the occasion by The Spitfire Academy. If you win the auction, you will fly in the rear seat of a Spitfire BS410, a genuine wartime aircraft that fought in World War II.

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: All of the proceeds will go to the Mark Long Trust and the RAF Benevolent Fund. One of the flights will take off from Prestwick on April 13, flying for just over an hour down to RAF Valley, accompanied by a Hawk T2 Jet as an escort. The auction for that flight currently stands at just under £8,000, and is expected to go much higher.

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.content: You can view the auction at spitfires.com/product-page/leg-5-prestwick-raf-valley . Organisers said: "This is the only opportunity anyone will ever have to fly these routes as part of an exclusive celebration of Spitfire history.

sourceReferences.citationText: serper-scottish-aviation – Bids to fly in rear seat of Spitfire from Prestwick Airport reach £8k
